# Network Services

## Overview

TUM provides essential network services through the IT Service Center and LRZ, including the Munich Scientific Network (MWN), VPN access, firewall services, identity management, and certificate services. These services ensure proper network connectivity and security for all TUM resources.

## Munich Scientific Network (MWN)

### Network Backbone

The Munich Scientific Network (MWN) connects all TUM buildings and provides high-speed connectivity:

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "MWN Network Architecture"
flowchart TB
    subgraph MWN["Munich Scientific Network"]
        Backbone["Network Backbone"]
    end

    subgraph Institutions["Connected Institutions"]
        TUM["TUM"]
        LMU["LMU"]
        HM["Hochschule München"]
        FH["FH Weihenstephan"]
    end

    subgraph External["External Networks"]
        DFN["German Research Network (DFN)"]
        Internet["Internet"]
    end

    TUM --> MWN
    LMU --> MWN
    HM --> MWN
    FH --> MWN

    MWN --> DFN
    DFN --> Internet
```

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Coverage** | All TUM buildings and campuses |
| **Upstream** | German Research Network (DFN) with internet access |
| **Partners** | LMU, Hochschule München, FH Weihenstephan |
| **Documentation** | [LRZ Network Documentation](https://doku.lrz.de/netz-10613425.html) |

### Local Network Planning

The IT Service Center and LRZ offer **free** assistance with local network planning:

- Network design consultation
- Infrastructure requirements analysis
- Capacity planning
- Integration with MWN
- Assistance with [connecting to MWN](http://www.lrz.de/services/netz/anschluss/)

### Network Maintenance

LRZ provides network infrastructure maintenance including:

| Service | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Active Components** | Switches, routers, access points |
| **Passive Components** | Cabling, patch panels |
| **Monitoring** | Network health monitoring |
| **Troubleshooting** | Issue diagnosis and resolution |

### Firewall / Routing Filters

Free firewall-as-a-service available for VLAN-based networks:

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Location** | Network transition points |
| **Requirement** | VLAN-based local network |
| **Cost** | Free |
| **Configuration** | Customizable rules |
| **Documentation** | [LRZ Firewall/pfSense](http://www.lrz.de/services/security/vfw-pfsense/) |

::: {.callout-note}
## VLAN Requirement
Firewall services require your network to be operated as a VLAN. Contact the IT Service Center for assistance with VLAN setup.
:::

### Open Network Access for Events

For conferences, workshops, and events requiring guest network access:

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Purpose** | Temporary guest access for event participants |
| **Requirements** | No VPN client needed by guests |
| **Validation** | Simplified or no validation |
| **Use Cases** | Conferences, workshops, symposia |
| **Documentation** | [LRZ MWN-Events](http://www.lrz.de/services/netz/wlan/mwn-events/) |

Request event network access through LRZ in advance of the event.

## DNS and Domain Services

### Domain Name System Management

DNS entries enable servers and services to be accessible by human-readable names instead of IP addresses.

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "DNS Resolution Flow"
flowchart LR
    User["User"]
    User --> |requests| Domain["server.department.tum.de"]
    Domain --> DNS["DNS Server"]
    DNS --> |resolves to| IP["IP Address"]
    IP --> Server["Department Server"]
```

### Domain and DNS Management

For DNS configuration and domain setup, contact the IT Service Center. See Chapter 4 (Internet Domains) for the full domain application process including eligibility requirements and approval workflow.

## DHCP Services

DHCP automatically assigns IP addresses to devices on the network. Network registration for static IP assignments is coordinated through the IT Service Center and LRZ network administration tools.

### Network Device Registration

To register devices on the network:

1. Collect device information (MAC address, device type, location)
2. Contact IT Service Center or use network registration tools
3. Device receives network configuration automatically

## VPN Services

### MWN VPN Overview

TUM provides VPN access through the Munich Scientific Network for remote access to TUM resources:

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "VPN Access Architecture"
flowchart TB
    subgraph Remote["Remote Locations"]
        Home["Home Office"]
        Travel["On the Road"]
        External["External Networks"]
    end

    subgraph VPN["MWN VPN Gateway"]
        Auth["TUM Authentication"]
        Tunnel["Encrypted Tunnel"]
    end

    subgraph TUM["TUM Network Resources"]
        Servers["Department Servers"]
        NAS["File Storage"]
        Services["Internal Services"]
    end

    Home --> VPN
    Travel --> VPN
    External --> VPN

    VPN --> TUM
```

### VPN Features

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Access** | Available to all TUM members |
| **Remote Access** | Access from home or while traveling |
| **Full Access** | Same as being on-site in MWN |
| **Secure** | Encrypted connection |

### Accessing TUM Resources via VPN

With VPN connected, users can:

- Access NAS fileservers (NFS, SMB)
- Connect to internal servers
- Use department-internal services
- Reach LDAP-authenticated resources

## Identity Management

### TUM Central Directory (LDAP)

The central TUM directory enables access to up-to-date personnel data for authentication, authorization, and data queries:

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "LDAP Integration Architecture"
flowchart TB
    subgraph Central["TUM Central Directory"]
        LDAP["LDAP Server"]
        Data["Personnel Data"]
    end

    subgraph Uses["Integration Options"]
        Auth["Authentication"]
        Authz["Authorization"]
        Query["Data Queries"]
        Sync["Data Synchronization"]
    end

    subgraph Systems["Your Systems"]
        Apps["Applications"]
        Services["Services"]
        Access["Access Control"]
    end

    LDAP --> Auth
    LDAP --> Authz
    LDAP --> Query
    Data --> Sync

    Auth --> Systems
    Authz --> Systems
    Query --> Systems
    Sync --> Systems
```

| Use Case | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| **Authentication** | Verify user identity against central directory |
| **Authorization** | Check user permissions and group memberships |
| **Data Queries** | Retrieve personnel information |
| **Data Sync** | Partially integrate data into local systems (requires approval) |
| **Documentation** | [TUM Authentication Services Wiki](https://wiki.tum.de/display/docs/Authentifizierungsdienste) |

### Active Directory (AD)

Central Active Directory service provided by LRZ for identity, rights, and resource management:

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Provider** | LRZ |
| **Provisioning** | Users and groups automatically from TUMonline |
| **Management** | Local administrators manage rights for their units |
| **Hardware** | Own hardware can be connected (e.g., department storage) |
| **Documentation** | [AD Documentation](https://wiki.tum.de/display/activedirectory/Dokumentation) |

```{mermaid}
%%| fig-cap: "Active Directory Flow"
flowchart LR
    TUMonline["TUMonline"]
    TUMonline --> |provisions| AD["Active Directory"]
    AD --> |manages| Users["Users"]
    AD --> |manages| Groups["Groups"]
    AD --> |manages| Computers["Computers"]
    AD --> |manages| Resources["IT Resources"]

    LocalAdmin["Local Admin"]
    LocalAdmin --> |manages rights| AD
```

### System Accounts

For non-personal computer systems (printers, scanners, automated services):

| Feature | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **Purpose** | Machine-to-machine authentication for devices |
| **Examples** | Printers, scanners, automated services |
| **Exchange** | Do NOT appear in TUM Exchange address book |
| **Email** | NOT mailboxes -- emails redirect to the responsible person |
| **Request** | Via online form in TUMonline |

::: {.callout-note}
## System Accounts Are Not Mailboxes
System Accounts cannot be used for receiving email. For shared email functionality (e.g., sekretariat@xyz.tum.de), request a Shared Mailbox via TUMonline Function Objects instead.
:::

## Certificates

### User Certificates (Nutzerzertifikate)

TUM issues X.509 user certificates through the DFN (German Research Network) and GÉANT PKI system:

| Aspect | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Standard** | X.509 certificates via DFN/GÉANT PKI |
| **Email Signing** | S/MIME protocol (Outlook, Thunderbird compatible) |
| **Email Encryption** | Both sender and recipient need S/MIME certificates |
| **Identity Verification** | Internet identity proof for websites and services |
| **Process** | 1) Request via DFN-PKI portal, 2) Import into email client |

::: {.callout-important}
## Limitations of User Certificates
TUM user certificates are **NOT suitable** for:

- Qualified electronic signatures (eIDAS)
- Document signing within TUM contexts requiring qualified signatures
- They do not meet qualified signature requirements
:::

### Server Certificates (Serverzertifikate)

TUM provides free certificates for servers:

| Option | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Let's Encrypt** (recommended) | Easier to set up, more reliable, faster, based on secure validation, extensive software support |
| **DFN-PKI** | Traditional certificates through German Research Network, free for TUM members |

::: {.callout-tip}
## Let's Encrypt Recommended
TUM actively recommends **Let's Encrypt** certificates for server services wherever technically feasible. They are easier to set up, more reliable, and faster than traditional DFN-PKI certificates. See [TUM Wiki](https://wiki.tum.de/x/8QWJRg) for setup instructions.
:::

## Network Troubleshooting Guide

### Common Issues

| Issue | Possible Cause | Solution |
|-------|---------------|----------|
| **No IP address** | Network registration missing | Check device registration |
| **DNS not resolving** | DNS entry missing | Contact IT Service Center |
| **VPN won't connect** | Credentials/config issue | Contact IT Service Center |
| **Can't reach resources** | Network segmentation | Verify VLAN configuration |

### Diagnostic Steps

1. **Verify physical connection** (cable, WiFi)
2. **Check IP configuration** (`ipconfig` / `ip addr`)
3. **Test DNS resolution** (`nslookup hostname`)
4. **Verify routing** (`traceroute`)
5. **Contact IT Service Center** if issues persist (+49.89.289.17123)

## Summary Table

| Service | Management | Request Method |
|---------|------------|----------------|
| **Network Planning** | IT Service Center / LRZ | it-support@tum.de |
| **DNS/Domains** | IT Service Center | Application form (see Ch. 4) |
| **Firewall** | LRZ | Via LRZ |
| **Event Network** | LRZ | Request in advance |
| **VPN** | LRZ | Self-service |
| **LDAP Integration** | TUM Central | IT Service Center |
| **Active Directory** | LRZ | Via TUMonline |
| **System Accounts** | TUMonline | Online form |
| **User Certificates** | DFN-PKI | Self-service portal |
| **Server Certificates** | Let's Encrypt / DFN-PKI | Self-service |
